2026-04-16T23:08:29.4463168Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T23:08:29.4463595Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T23:08:29.4464144Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T23:08:29.4464457Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T23:08:29.4464979Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-16T23:10:34.2485468Z **Gaps found:**

2026-04-16T23:11:18.2350987Z      - **Authentication section is empty** - no content under "Authenticate the Client"
2026-04-16T23:11:18.2351949Z      - **Next steps section is empty** - no links to additional resources or documentation
2026-04-16T23:11:18.2353305Z      - **Key concepts** section doesn't mention `ResultKind` enum or important `ResourceGraphQuery` properties like `ETag` for optimistic concurrency
2026-04-16T23:11:18.2354970Z      - No documentation about the `FromExisting` static method for referencing existing resources
2026-04-16T23:11:18.2356020Z      - The example snippet doesn't demonstrate `FromExisting` or `Tags` usage
2026-04-16T23:11:18.2356637Z   
2026-04-16T23:11:18.2358991Z   The most critical gaps are the empty authentication and next steps sections—these are foundational for any provisioning library. Beyond that, the key concepts need to cover the `ResultKind` enum and `ETag` behavior, and the examples should show how to work with existing resources and tags.

2026-04-16T23:11:18.4429508Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T23:11:05.192521351Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58068) by @ArcturusZhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nA new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library was added (1.0.0-beta.1) providing declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Resource Graph shared queries. The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate)\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries\n- **`Key concepts` section** doesn't document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier)\n- **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md`\n\n**1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`):\n\nReplace:\n```markdown\n### Authenticate the Client\n\n## Key concepts\n```\n\nWith:\n```markdown\n### Authenticate the Client\n\nNo authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://aka.ms/azure-dev). Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library.\n\n## Key concepts\n```\n\n**1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** — add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\n```\n\nWith:\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet.  You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\n\n### ResourceGraphQuery\n\n`ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/resource-graph/overview). Key writable properties include:\n\n| Property | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph |\n| `Description` | Human-readable description of the query |\n| `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite |\n| `Tags` | Freeform resource tags |\n\nThe following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment:\n\n| Property | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) |\n| `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification |\n| `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier |\n\n### Referencing existing resources\n\nUse the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one:\n\n```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\nResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\n```\n\n\u003c/details_end\u003e\n```\n\n**1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** — replace the empty section:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\n- Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/resource-graph/overview)\n- Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/governance/resource-graph/samples/starter)\n- Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://aka.ms/azure-dev) to deploy your infrastructure\n- Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries](https://www.nuget.org/packages?q=Azure.Provisioning) for other Azure services\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n**2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`.\n\nAdd a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`:\n\n```csharp\n[Test]\npublic void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery()\n{\n    Infrastructure infra = new();\n\n    #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\n    ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery));\n    #endregion\n\n    infra.Add(existingQuery);\n}\n```\n\n**2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above):\n\n````markdown\n```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting\n```\n````\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty:\n\n| Section | Status |\n|---------|--------|\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete |\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete |\n| Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1a |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Minimal — expand with Step 1b |\n| Examples | ✅ Has snippet |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete |\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty — add content from Step 1c |\n| Contributing | ✅ Complete |\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` — injects snippet code from test `#region` blocks into README placeholders; verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` — no public API changes expected, but run to confirm\n5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready
